description: One who gathers wild food; a person who harvests what nature provides. 'Nunoa' names who finds abundance—knowing where the wild feeds.
sound_symbolism: The word seeks close to the earth. 'Nu' begins with grounded presence, the forager's attention on what is here, now, underfoot. 'Noa' settles with knowing, the quiet recognition of what the land offers. A word that stays low and attentive.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'nunoa mua shelira nuora sholei.' (The forager gathers food in the forest.) Gathers from 'shelira' (forest) and 'pelowa' (meadow). Related to 'telo' (farmer) as gatherer to cultivator.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Wild harvest—sustainable gathering that supports rather than depletes.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Forager as knower—deep knowledge of what the land offers freely.
